decks.notes: Utilize land ramp to quickly assemble the mana needed to cast Primeval Titan and combo off with Scapeshift to put multiple Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle and Mountain lands into play to deal massive damage to the opponent in one turn. The deck also has alternative win conditions with Urza's Saga and Amulet of Vigor to create huge threats like Aftermath Analyst and Cultivator Colossus. Focus on ramping up and setting up the combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful and explosive finish.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total. The deck also includes burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge to deal direct damage and clear the way for attacks. Additionally, cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide card advantage and board presence. The inclusion of versatile lands like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand ensures consistent mana fixing, allowing the deck to maintain its aggressive strategy throughout the game.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana through rituals and other spells to cast powerful combo cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot. The goal is to chain together spells to continuously cast and recast cards from the graveyard using Past in Flames. Ruby Medallion helps reduce the cost of spells, while cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Wish provide additional support and flexibility. By efficiently mana ramping and comboing off, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with damage before they can react.

The deck focuses on assembling a toolbox of creatures to combo off using Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Yawgmoth, Thran Physician. It includes resilient creatures like Young Wolf and Walking Ballista, as well as graveyard recursion with Endurance and Underground Mortuary. Malevolent Rumble adds removal, while Dredger's Insight helps with card advantage. The deck also includes tutors like Green Sun's Zenith and Spymaster's Vault to find key pieces, and some utility creatures like Grist, the Hunger Tide and Strangleroot Geist. Overall, the deck aims to create powerful synergies and win through a combo finish.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combination of Sowing Mycospawn and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate a powerful creature with Basking Broodscale. By using cards like Malevolent Rumble and Unholy Heat to control the board, along with Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths to apply pressure, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a lethal combo finish. The deck also includes mana acceleration with cards like Grove of the Burnwillows and Eldrazi Temple, as well as protection with Boseiju, Who Endures and Cavern of Souls.

The main focus of this deck is to use Eldrazi creatures to control the game while also ramping up mana quickly. By utilizing cards like Malevolent Rumble, Kozilek's Command, and Fight Rigging, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect its creatures. With powerful threats like Ugin, Emrakul, and Ulamog, the deck can quickly overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. The Eldrazi Temple and Ugin's Labyrinth provide additional mana support to help cast these powerful creatures early in the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing cascade effects from cards like Shardless Agent and Violent Outburst to quickly and efficiently cast Living End from the graveyard. The goal is to continuously cycle creatures into the graveyard, then bring them all back onto the battlefield at once with Living End to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruption and control elements like Force of Negation and Subtlety to protect the combo and deal with opposing threats. The key strategy is to set up the graveyard while controlling the board, then unleash a game-winning cascade combo with Living End.

This deck focuses on aggressive play, utilizing a combination of creatures and spells to quickly overpower the opponent. The key strategy involves using low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Orcish Bowmasters to apply early pressure, while also having access to removal spells like Fatal Push and Galvanic Discharge to clear the way. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls provide additional buffs and support for the team, helping to maintain board presence and push for victory. With a mix of creatures, removal, and support cards, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and powerful offense.

This deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Combo, using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to quickly tutor for and play powerful creatures like Allosaurus Rider and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. Glittering Wish acts as a versatile tutor, allowing access to various silver bullet cards in the sideboard. The deck also includes defensive cards like Pact of Negation and Consign to Memory to protect the combo and disrupt opponents. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and synergies.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming your opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing fast creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, along with cheap burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, you can quickly reduce your opponent's life total to zero. The deck also includes pump spells like Mutagenic Growth and Monstrous Rage to help push through damage. Utilizing card selection with Preordain and Mishra's Bauble, as well as card draw with Slickshot Show-Off, this deck aims to win the game before your opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on using Eldrazi creatures to ramp up mana and control the game through powerful spells and abilities. It aims to quickly cast large Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog or Emrakul while also controlling the opponent's board through spells like Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command. The deck also includes mana ramp with cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate its game plan. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around quickly playing big threats and disrupting the opponent's plans with control elements.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. It also utilizes cards like Faithful Mending and Thoughtseize to control the game and disrupt opponent's strategies. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide card advantage and filtering, while Solitude offers removal and protection. The deck aims to combo off and win the game in a single explosive turn, utilizing the graveyard as a resource to maximize its potential.

This deck focuses on utilizing creatures with sacrifice abilities and a toolbox of different creature options to create powerful combos. By sacrificing creatures like Cauldron Familiar, City of Brass, and Samwise Gamgee, the player can generate value and trigger effects like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah's flip ability or Goblin Bombardment's direct damage. Chord of Calling allows the player to search for specific creatures when needed, while cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional utility and protection. Ultimately, the goal is to assemble the right pieces for a game-winning play.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler to quickly pressure the opponent and deal damage. It also includes spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board and clear the way for attacks. The deck aims to be fast and aggressive, aiming to win the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize. The mixture of creatures, burn spells, and card draw allows for a versatile and aggressive playstyle that can overwhelm opponents in a fast-paced game.

The main concept of this Aggro - Hollow One Modern deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures to bring out powerful creatures like Hollow One and Vengevine early in the game. By using cards like Burning Inquiry and Goblin Lore, the deck aims to discard cards rapidly and enable graveyard triggers. The deck also includes some burn spells like Lightning Bolt for removal and reach. Overall, the strategy focuses on fast-paced aggression and graveyard synergies to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly.

The primary focus of this deck is to maintain control of the game through various counter spells, removal cards, and disruptive spells. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually gaining advantage through card draw and selection. Using cards like Snapcaster Mage and Cling to Dust, the player can utilize their graveyard as a resource. The deck also includes cards that deal with problematic permanents, such as Field of Ruin and Sheoldred's Edict. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition and resource management.
